Fix a zero-error update code of blocklength n u for the message set K : an encoder enc : K → X n u and decoder dec : Y n u →K such that for every a ∈ K , if X n u = enc( a ) is transmitted then dec( Y n u ) = a almost surely. 
We construct ˜ A by simulating A as a virtual clean algorithm. Partition physical time into blocks B i ≜ { ( i -1) n u +1 , . . . , in u } for i = 1 , 2 , . . . , and let t i ≜ in u denote the last slot of block i . 
At virtual step i , the simulated algorithm A outputs an arm request a i ∈ K as a measurable function of its past virtual rewards. During physical block B i , the learner transmits the lengthn u codeword enc( a i ) over the actuation channel. By the assumed timing model, the agent executes the decoded arm immediately upon decoding , i.e., in the last slot t i the executed arm equals a i . Define the virtual reward fed back to A at step i to be the physical reward r t i observed in that last slot. All other rewards inside the block are ignored.

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the virtual algorithm A simulates the physical arm commands and how it relates to the zero-error capacity of the underlying DMC?
finetune_sample.output: The simulated algorithm A constructs a virtual clean algorithm by partitioning physical time into blocks and assigning a last slot t_i to each block. During physical block B_i, the learner transmits the length n_u codeword enc(a_i) over the actuation channel, which is decoded immediately upon decoding in the last slot t_i. The virtual reward fed back to A at step i is the physical reward r_t_i observed in that last slot, ignoring all other rewards inside the block. This simulation relates to the zero-error capacity of the underlying DMC by utilizing a zero-error update code with a blocklength n_u to encode and decode the message set K.

clean_text: From coarse to fine-grained steering. We vary the amount of detail provided to SteerViT via the text prompt from coarse supercategories (e.g., 'mug', 'shoe'), object names (e.g., 'white ECCV mug'), to comprehensive MLLMgenerated descriptions of each instance's visual appearance. As shown in Fig. 8, the semantics of the steered representations are sensitive to the prompt specificity. When conditioning is too coarse, the model overlooks fine-grained cues necessary for discriminating instances within an object category, yielding slightly worse performance than vanilla DINOv2 ( 27 . 9% vs. 29 . 6% PR-AUC). Enriching the prompt with instance-level descriptions substantially boosts performance to 58 . 1% PR-AUC, surpassing custom DINOv2 variants fine-tuned on synthetic task-specific data ( 48 . 0% PR-AUC) and nearly closing the gap between zeroshot and fine-tuned DINOv2 on retrieval ( 77 . 3% vs. 79 . 6% NDCG). This result is particularly noteworthy given that DINOv2 fine-tuning is

finetune_sample.output: The PR-AUC performance of SteerViT is 27.9% when conditioning on coarse supercategories, and 58.1% when conditioning on comprehensive MLLM-generated descriptions.

clean_text: Vector autoregressions (VARs) and Structural VARs (Sims, 1980; Stock & Watson, 2018) introduce identifying assumptions (e.g., via instruments or narrative restrictions) to obtain interpretable shocks, but they rely on linearity and scale poorly to high dimensions. Deep forecasters, such as iTransformer (Liu et al., 2023) and TimeMixer (Wang et al., 2024a), and state-space architectures such as S4 (Gu et al., 2022) and Mamba (Gu & Dao, 2023), offer strong predictive performance but either lack stochastic latent factors or yield deterministic embeddings that do not admit a clear factor interpretation. Deep Dynamic Factor Models (Andreini et al., 2020) and Deep Kalman Filters (Krishnan et al., 2015) combine probabilistic temporal structure with flexible nonlinear mappings, yet they do not provide identifiability guarantees, so that the learned factors remain defined only up to equivalence classes that can include rotations. Our model retains explicit stochastic dynamics and a

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the main limitation of traditional deep forecasters in terms of interpretability, as highlighted by the authors in this paper?
finetune_sample.output: Traditional deep forecasters, such as iTransformer and TimeMixer, lack stochastic latent factors or yield deterministic embeddings that do not admit a clear factor interpretation. This limitation arises because they either rely on linearity or scale poorly to high dimensions, making it challenging to obtain interpretable shocks.

clean_text: Software implementation and hardware platforms. We implement the GPU-based Zak-OTFS processing pipeline in approximately 3,200 lines of Python code. PyTorch is used as the primary backend for general tensor computation, and OpenAI Triton is used to develop custom GPU kernels for largescale matrix operations and performance-critical components, such as MVMs and GEMMs. We evaluate our implementation of the end-to-end Zak-OTFS receiver processing primarily on an NVIDIA H200 GPU. We also consider multiple NVIDIA GPU platforms, including Jetson Orin, RTX 6000 Ada, and A100, to provide a comprehensive performance comparison. To understand the impact of the hardware architecture, we also implement the same Zak-OTFS processing pipeline in C++ using the Armadillo library and evaluate it on a Dell PowerEdge R750 server, equipped with two Intel Xeon Gold 6348 processors, each with 28 physical cores (56 threads) running at 2.6 GHz base frequency. Table III provides the detailed

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the Zak-OTFS receiver processing pipeline is optimized for real-time processing using compact matrix operations, branchless iterative equalizer, and structured sparse channel matrix?
finetune_sample.output: The Zak-OTFS receiver processing pipeline is optimized for real-time processing by leveraging compact matrix operations to reduce computational overhead. This is achieved through the use of a structured sparse channel matrix, which enables significant reductions in memory requirements. Additionally, a branchless iterative equalizer is employed to further minimize latency and improve performance. These optimizations enable low-latency processing that consistently meets the 99.9-th percentile real-time processing deadline.

clean_text: Our Residual Quantized Variational Autoencoder (RQ-VAE) follows the TIGER (Rajput et al., 2023) framework with carefully designed architectural specifications to ensure effective quantization of semantic representations. The encoder architecture consists of a 3-layer Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) with hidden dimensions of , utilizing ReLU activation functions and applying a dropout rate of 0.1 between layers. The residual quantization mechanism employs four codebook layers, each containing 256 entries with 32-dimensional codes. This hierarchical quantization approach enables fine-grained representation of semantic information while maintaining discrete tokenization properties essential for language model integration. We trained the model for 20,000 epochs to achieve a high codebook utilization rate and minimize collision rates. To further prevent collisions where multiple items map to identical sequences of semantic IDs, we employed the Sinkhorn-Knopp trick used by

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the Sinkhorn-Knopp trick is used in the RQ-VAE model to prevent collisions where multiple items map to identical sequences of semantic IDs?
finetune_sample.output: The Sinkhorn-Knopp trick is employed in the RQ-VAE model to prevent collisions by iteratively updating the weights of the codebook layers. This method ensures that each sequence of semantic IDs maps to a unique set of codes, thereby reducing collision rates and maintaining discrete tokenization properties essential for language model integration.

clean_text: the 'forward' action moves a sprite one tile forward in the direction it is facing. This is particularly interesting in our setup as subject orientations can only be inferred from raw video frames; hence, the model has to comprehend visual information to get the correct action outcome, rather than learning absolute directional instructions like 'move up'. We generate game-specific text descriptions using an LLM from the Melting Pot paper and videos of each game (full details in Supplementary). For training, we gather 2,000 videos per game at a resolution of 512 × 512. We generate diverse rollouts for each game by executing either random actions or pretrained policies at each step t . For evaluation, we collect 230 rollouts (5 per game). As completely random actions result in players staying close to their initial positions, we enforced a i t ∼ { Move,Turn } for t < 2 to leave its initial state, and at least one subject is assigned an 'Interact' action for t ≥ 2 .

finetune_sample.instruction: How do subject orientations in ActionParty models get inferred from raw video frames, and what is the role of spatial biasing mechanism?
finetune_sample.output: In ActionParty, subject orientations are inferred from raw video frames as the model has to comprehend visual information to determine the correct action outcome. Since absolute directional instructions like 'move up' cannot be used, the model relies on understanding the sprite's orientation in relation to its surroundings. The spatial biasing mechanism plays a crucial role in this process by jointly modeling state tokens and video latents, allowing the model to disentangle global video frame rendering from individual action-controlled subject updates.

clean_text:
We train on a mixture of referential segmentation and grounding datasets to ensure diversity in both visual domains and textual expression styles (see Fig. 16): RefCOCO, RefCOCO+, RefCOCOg provide referring expressions grounded in COCO images. RefCOCO+ excludes spatial language (e.g., 'left of'), encouraging the model to rely on appearance cues, while RefCOCOg contains longer, more descriptive expressions that exercise the model's capacity for detailed textual understanding. 
LVIS uses the same underlying COCO images but also considers fine-grained and longtail object categories. 
Visual Genome (preprocessed following MDeTr ) contributes region descriptions paired with bounding boxes across densely annotated scenes. These descriptions span a broader vocabulary and more complex spatial relationships than the RefCOCO family, increasing the diversity of text conditioning signals. We use SAM2 to convert bounding boxes to binary segmentation masks.

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the authors ensured diversity in both visual domains and textual expression styles during training?
finetune_sample.output: The authors ensured diversity in both visual domains and textual expression styles by training on a mixture of referential segmentation and grounding datasets. This includes using datasets such as RefCOCO, RefCOCO+, RefCOCOg, LVIS, Visual Genome, and SAM2 to provide a range of visual and textual inputs. The use of different datasets with varying levels of complexity and diversity helps to exercise the model's capacity for detailed textual understanding and appearance cues.

clean_text:
Ethics. All user data is anonymized and used with explicit consent for research purposes. 
Baselines. We compare HabitatAgent against six baselines representing common design choices: B1: Monolithic RAG , a single-prompt dense-retrieval system without memory or verification; B2: Dense+Rerank , a dense retriever (BGE) with a reranker, but without memory or verification; B3: GraphRAG-Fixed , graph-based retrieval for all queries without adaptive routing; B4: LLM-Ranker , which ranks a large candidate set without explicit verification; B5: Self-RAG , which performs error detection followed by full regeneration; and B6: RuleVerifier , a rule-based verifier that refuses responses when errors are detected. For a fair comparison, all methods use the same underlying LLM and, where applicable, the same candidate pool. 
Metrics We report metrics that reflect both recommendation utility and consultation reliability: 
- Recommendation quality: nDCG@5.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary goal of using multiple specialized agent roles in HabitatAgent, and how do these roles contribute to an auditable and reliable workflow for end-to-end housing consultation?
finetune_sample.output: HabitatAgent uses multiple specialized agent roles - Memory, Retrieval, Generation, and Validation - to provide an auditable and reliable workflow for end-to-end housing consultation. The Memory Agent maintains user memory through internal stages for constraint extraction, memory fusion, and verification-gated updates. The Retrieval Agent performs hybrid vector--graph retrieval (GraphRAG) to retrieve relevant information. The Generation Agent produces evidence-referenced recommendations and explanations. Finally, the Validation Agent applies multi-tier verification and targeted remediation to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the consultation process.

clean_text:
Operating at a Swarm Batch Size of 64 (64,000 continuous packets), the Zero-Copy IPC bridge facilitated an aggregate inference latency of 1.6 ms per batch (effectively 25 µ s per 1,000-packet sequence). This establishes a theoretical computational ceiling of 40 Million Packets Per Second (Mpps), heavily outperforming traditional Python-bound ingestion pipelines. 
However, empirical stress testing at Batch 256 (simulating 128+ Mpps throughput) revealed a hard hardware limit in cloud environments. Latency spiked non-linearly to 7.1 ms. This degradation was entirely localized to the virtualized PCIe Gen 4 bus attempting to transfer 5.86 MB of shared memory per strike. This confirms that while the AEGIS computational engine scales efficiently to 100 Gbps line-rates, physical deployment requires bare-metal hardware utilizing NVIDIA GPUDirect RDMA and ConnectX-7 SmartNICs to bypass System RAM entirely and avoid the I/O transfer bottleneck.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary limitation encountered by AEGIS when scaling to high throughput in cloud environments?
finetune_sample.output: AEGIS's scalability is limited in cloud environments due to a hard hardware limit, which causes latency to spike non-linearly at high batch sizes. This degradation is attributed to the virtualized PCIe Gen 4 bus attempting to transfer large amounts of shared memory, resulting in an I/O transfer bottleneck.

clean_text:
Early vs. late fusion. We interleave gated cross-attention within ViT layers (early fusion). Late fusion (Tab. 4, row 3) adds text only after the final ViT layer. While late fusion (row 3) also achieves high steerability (93.3) and higher FGCLS (91.8 vs. 87.7), it reduces PODS performance dramatically (36.6 vs. 58.1). 
This illustrates the importance of early fusion for fine-grained vision-language modeling, with the gap vanishing (26.5 vs. 27.9) when conditioning on coarse supercategory prompts. 
Role of gating. Removing the zero-initialized tanh gate (row 4) reduces FGCLS, CORE, and PODS by 4.2, 1.4, and 11.0 points below SteerViT (row 2), showing that ungated cross-attention disrupts frozen features. 
Language projector. A two-layer MLP projects RoBERTa features to the ViT space (Sec. 3.1). Replacing it with a linear projector (row 5) lowers FG-CLS by 1.0 and PODS by 1.7 points, indicating that the MLP better aligns modalities.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary difference between early fusion and late fusion in vision-language models, as illustrated by the experiment results?
finetune_sample.output: The primary difference between early fusion and late fusion in vision-language models lies in when text is integrated with visual features. Early fusion involves injecting text directly into the layers of the visual encoder via lightweight cross-attention, whereas late fusion adds text only after the final ViT layer. This distinction affects the performance of the model on tasks such as fine-grained vision-language modeling and anomaly detection.

clean_text: We introduce Steerable Visual Representations (SteerViT), a new class of visual representations that equip any pretrained visual encoder with the ability to steer its features with natural language. SteerViT inverts the MLLM paradigm by conditioning the visual encoder on language. By interleaving lightweight crossattention layers into frozen ViT blocks, our method steers both global and local features with text while preserving the base ViT's representation quality, achieving a Pareto improvement over prior approaches with only ∼ 21M trainable parameters. Across personalized object discrimination and industrial anomaly segmentation, our method matches or surpasses dedicated methods without taskspecific training, generalizing across multiple ViT backbones. These results suggest that text can serve as a lightweight, post-hoc steering mechanism that extends the capabilities of rich vision encoders to new domains without fine-tuning.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the main innovation of Steerable Visual Representations compared to existing vision-language models?
finetune_sample.output: Steerable Visual Representations (SteerViT) introduces a new class of visual representations that equip any pretrained visual encoder with the ability to steer its features with natural language. This is achieved by interleaving lightweight cross-attention layers into frozen ViT blocks, conditioning the visual encoder on language and preserving the base ViT's representation quality.

clean_text:
We evaluate ActionParty on video outputs from the Melting Pot benchmark , a challenging suite of 46 2D multi-agent games. We learn a unified action space that applies to all games, and our model maintains precise persubject control over up to seven players. Thanks to the separate modeling of subject states, we significantly outperform text-only baselines in action-following accuracy and identity consistency. Beyond pure video generation, we show that the co-generated subject states accurately follow the moving subjects in the video, even through complex interactions and changes in character states. In summary, we make the following contributions: 
- -We propose ActionParty, a novel autoregressive video generator for generative game simulation. We jointly model video frames and the state of each subject and introduce attention masking to enforce action-subject correspondence to enable consistent multi-subject action control.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the specific contribution of attention masking in ActionParty, and how does it enable consistent multi-subject action control?
finetune_sample.output: ActionParty introduces attention masking to enforce action-subject correspondence, which enables consistent multi-subject action control by preventing the model from mixing or confusing different subjects' actions. This allows for robust autoregressive tracking of subjects through complex interactions.

clean_text:
While previous sections revealed steerability for SteerViT and OV localization, this is only useful if it preserves the downstream transfer, an important property of good visual representations. A naïve solution that overwrites visual features with text yields perfect steerability but destroys the underlying representation. 
To assess this trade-off, we contrast CORE performance (cf. Sec. 4.1) with vision-centric downstream tasks intended to measure representational quality. In addition to training linear probes on global features across three fine-grained classification datasets (ImageWoof , Waterbirds , StanfordCars ), binary object-of-interest segmentation on ADE20k gauges the dense encoding performance. Here, promptable models are conditioned on the superclass (e.g., 'dog' for ImageWoof) and the ADE20k class name, respectively. For Fig. 2 and 7, both scores are averaged. Additional details are in supplement D.3.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary concern when using Steerable Visual Representations, specifically in terms of preserving downstream transfer?
finetune_sample.output: The primary concern with using Steerable Visual Representations is that perfect steerability can destroy the underlying representation quality. This trade-off must be carefully considered to ensure that the visual features remain useful for downstream tasks.

clean_text:
Query-agnostic encoders collapse to salient concepts. Query-agnostic encoders fail at conditional retrieval because their features collapse to the dominant scene concept (Figure 5). MAE barely exceeds random chance ( 20% ) and DINOv2, despite its object-centric representations, achieves only 44% acc@1. Cross-modal visual encoders (CLIP, SigLIP) also perform poorly despite operating in a shared vision-language space. In contrast, SteerViT achieves 96% retrieval accuracy, confirming that text conditioning shifts the global representation from the scene level ('kitchen') to the queried concept ('fruit bowl'). 
Figure 5a illustrates this on 'kitchen' scenes for three objects via UMAP . DINOv2 embeddings (top left) show no object separability, whereas our textconditioned embeddings form distinct clusters for different text prompts. This confirms that SteerViT can reorganize its embedding space around the queried concept.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary issue that query-agnostic encoders face when it comes to conditional retrieval, according to the SteerViT paper?
finetune_sample.output: Query-agnostic encoders collapse to salient concepts, which leads to poor performance in conditional retrieval tasks. This collapse results from their failure to capture the specific concept or object of interest within an image.

clean_text:
We examine how SteerViT routes and aggregates global representations via attention to query-relevant tokens. For this, we construct a benchmark by stitching together four images from PASCAL-VOC into a single 2 × 2 mosaic, resulting in a total of 363 composite images with reduced saliency of each primary subject. The [CLS] -to-patch attention scores in the final self-attention block highlight how global attention is redirected to regions specified by text prompt. 
Qualitative analysis. As revealed by Fig. 6, DINOv2's attention map focuses primarily on the prominent 'pony' and 'airplane' entities, confirming the saliency bias hypothesized in Sec. 1. In contrast, SteerViT conditioned on 'person' routes attention to the barely visible person in the top-left. When prompted with 'potted plant', it distributes attention among the class instances in the bottom-right image. Additional qualitative examples are in Figure 14.

finetune_sample.instruction: What specific type of attention mechanism is used in SteerViT to steer global representations towards query-relevant tokens?
finetune_sample.output: SteerViT uses a lightweight cross-attention mechanism to inject text directly into the layers of the visual encoder, allowing for early fusion and steering of global representations via attention to query-relevant tokens.

clean_text: The iteration budget is determined offline using the BERdriven criterion described above, and a fixed-iteration execution model is adopted during runtime. While condition-based early termination is common in CPU implementations, it is inherently inefficient for the GPU's SIMT architecture due to the costly global synchronization and host-device interactions to decide whether to launch the subsequent demodulation kernel. Predetermined termination avoids unnecessary runtime monitoring while enabling predictable performance with efficient hardware utilization: executing a fixed number of iterations allows the computation to be organized into a fully GPU-resident pipeline with minimal host intervention. For example, for a DD domain grid size of (128 , 32) with 10 conjugate gradient iterations, eliminating CPU-GPU synchronization reduces the equalization time from 0.65 ms to 0.54 ms on average, yielding a 17% speedup.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the effect of using a fixed-iteration execution model versus condition-based early termination on GPU performance in the Zak-OTFS receiver design?
finetune_sample.output: Using a fixed-iteration execution model avoids unnecessary runtime monitoring and enables predictable performance with efficient hardware utilization, resulting in a 17% speedup compared to condition-based early termination. This is achieved by eliminating CPU-GPU synchronization, which reduces the equalization time from 0.65 ms to 0.54 ms on average for a DD domain grid size of (128 , 32) with 10 conjugate gradient iterations.

clean_text:
Datasets and task. We use five real-world multivariate time-series datasets: ETTh1 and ETTh2 (electricity transformer temperature, hourly), ETTm1 and ETTm2 (15-minute), and Weather. For each dataset we train on a fixed train/val split and evaluate out-of-sample forecasts at horizons H ∈ { 96 , 192 , 336 , 720 } . Predictions are made in standardized (scaled) space: we fit a scaler on the training set and evaluate metrics without inverse-transforming, so that MSE and CRPS are comparable across variables and datasets. 
Baselines. iVDFM is compared to: iTransformer (Liu et al., 2023), TimeMixer (Wang et al., 2024a), TimeXer (Wang et al., 2024b), DDFM (Andreini et al., 2020), and MLPMultivariate (MLP). All baselines are trained and evaluated on the same splits and horizons. iVDFM uses fixed perdataset hyperparameters (no Optuna at evaluation time) for reproducibility; see config and ablation (Appendix E) for details.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the range of horizons used for out-of-sample forecasts in the experiments on real-world multivariate time-series datasets?
finetune_sample.output: The experiment uses a fixed train/val split and evaluates out-of-sample forecasts at horizons H ∈ {96, 192, 336, 720}.

clean_text: In recent years, large language models (LLMs) have been increasingly applied to specialized vertical industries, such as finance and healthcare. Examples include BloombergGPT , FinGPT , and FinRobot , which leverage LLMs to assist in complex decision-making tasks in their respective domains. These systems highlight the potential of LLMs in providing tailored solutions, demonstrating how they can be employed for high-precision, domainspecific tasks. More broadly, LLM-based multi-agent systems have emerged as a general paradigm for decomposing complex tasks into specialized roles . These directions are highly relevant to housing consultation, where users often impose relational constraints such as transit access, school districts, or location dependencies. In addition, recent housing-related benchmarks and datasets further highlight the importance of grounded evidence and reliable decision support in this domain .

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the Memory Agent in HabitatAgent maintains multi-layer user memory through internal stages for constraint extraction, memory fusion, and verification-gated updates?
finetune_sample.output: The Memory Agent in HabitatAgent uses internal stages to maintain multi-layer user memory. These stages include constraint extraction, where it identifies relevant constraints from the user's input; memory fusion, which combines information from multiple layers of memory to form a cohesive representation of the user's preferences; and verification-gated updates, which ensure that any changes made to the user's memory are verified against the existing constraints before being applied. This multi-stage approach enables the Memory Agent to effectively manage complex user memories and provide reliable recommendations.

clean_text:
While theoretical detection rates are critical, the practical deployment of deep learning models in operational network environments often fails due to integration friction. Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) engines typically operate at the kernel level to intercept high-speed traffic, whereas Python-based neural networks introduce severe user-space context-switching delays. 
To ensure AEGIS is viable for real-world perimeter defense, the architecture is designed to interface with modern packet processing frameworks like eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) or DPDK (Data Plane Development Kit). In an operational deployment, a lightweight eBPF probe extracts the 6-dimensional physics vector x i = [ S i , ∆ t i , D i , W i , F i , P i ] directly within the Linux kernel. This array is then asynchronously streamed to the GPU-accelerated AEGIS inference engine via zero-copy shared memory rings (e.g., AF XDP).

finetune_sample.instruction: How does AEGIS's design to interface with modern packet processing frameworks like eBPF or DPDK address integration friction in practical deployment of deep learning models in operational network environments?
finetune_sample.output: AEGIS's architecture is designed to interface with modern packet processing frameworks like eBPF or DPDK by using a lightweight eBPF probe that extracts the 6-dimensional physics vector within the Linux kernel. This array is then asynchronously streamed to the GPU-accelerated AEGIS inference engine via zero-copy shared memory rings, minimizing user-space context-switching delays and ensuring seamless integration with operational network environments.

The training pipeline consists of a supervised setup using a mean squared error loss optimized Adam with a learning rate of 1 e -3 , weight decay of 1 e -2 , and a batch size of 64. We also schedule the learning rate to stabilize the training with linear scaling warm-up for 8% of the steps and a cosine decay schedule after the initial warm-up period. We use a latent dimension of 256/512 for the Mamba/LSTM sequence processing modules, and the dimension of the lifted Koopman space is determined by an expansion factor on the

input space dimension ranging from 1.5-2x depending on test system size. All models are scaled to equalize the number of parameters, while maintaining the dimensionality of the Koopman measurement functions across models.

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the learning rate schedule in the MambaDSSE framework is designed to stabilize training, and what are its components?
finetune_sample.output: The learning rate schedule in the MambaDSSE framework uses a combination of linear scaling warm-up and cosine decay to stabilize training. The initial 8% of steps feature a linear scaling warm-up, where the learning rate increases from 1e-3 to its maximum value over this period. After the warm-up, the learning rate follows a cosine decay schedule, which gradually decreases the learning rate back down to 1e-3. This schedule helps to prevent overshooting and oscillations during training, allowing the model to converge more reliably.

clean_text: The base language model employs Qwen3-0.6B with hidden dimension of 1024. The model architecture comprises 28 transformer layers supporting a maximum context length of 32,768 tokens. This configuration provides sufficient capacity for processing sequential recommendation tasks while maintaining computational efficiency. Parameter-efficient fine-tuning is implemented through Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation (QLoRA) with a rank of 8 and alpha value of 32. The LoRA adaptation applies a dropout rate of 0.05 and targets key projection matrices including q proj, k proj, v proj, o proj, gate proj, up proj, and down proj. We also set LoRA modules to be saved as embed tokens and lm head, so that only the embedding layer and the language modeling head are preserved during training while other modules can remain frozen. This configuration enables efficient adaptation while preserving pre-trained knowledge.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary concern with using mean initialization for new vocabulary tokens in LMs, according to the authors of the paper?
finetune_sample.output: The standard practice initializes new tokens as the mean of existing vocabulary embeddings, which collapses all new tokens into a degenerate subspace, erasing inter-token distinctions that subsequent fine-tuning struggles to fully recover. This collapse leads to poor performance and highlights the importance of improving token initialization strategies.

clean_text: Machine learning in finance is a rapidly growing field that is gradually transforming how financial institutions operate and make decisions. One of its most promising sub-fields is the use of deep and reinforcement learning (RL) for quantitative finance applications, where examples include derivatives pricing, portfolio optimization and algorithmic trading. RL adopts a model-free perspective: agents choose actions in an unknown environment by learning from interactions, and they optimally balance the exploration of the environment and the exploitation of the learned knowledge. RL has shown great success in various fields, including playing games such as Go [Silver et al., 2016, 2017], training large language models [Ouyang et al., 2022; Bai et al., 2022; Guo et al., 2025], controlling robotics [Levine et al., 2016; Haarnoja et al., 2019], managing energy [Chen et al., 2022], and many more.

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how machine learning in finance is used for quantitative applications, specifically in derivatives pricing and algorithmic trading?
finetune_sample.output: Machine learning in finance is applied to quantitative applications such as derivatives pricing and algorithmic trading through the use of deep reinforcement learning (RL) models. RL enables agents to learn from interactions with an unknown environment, balancing exploration and exploitation to optimally balance risk and reward. This approach has shown great success in various fields, including playing games like Go, training large language models, controlling robotics, managing energy, and more.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the main challenge that ActionParty aims to address in existing video diffusion models?
finetune_sample.output: ActionParty tackles a fundamental issue of action binding in existing video diffusion models, which struggle to associate specific actions with their corresponding subjects. This limitation restricts these models to single-agent settings and fails to control multiple agents simultaneously in a scene.

- We then propose two Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) solutions that learn optimal policies for both noncooperative and cooperative settings, while adhering to the system dynamics and different constraints.
- To validate our solutions, rigorous testing and experimentation were performed on both solutions and showed significant improvements in request completion, fulfillment, resource balance, overall system efficiency, and adaptability compared to baseline methods. 
Following this section, the paper proceeds as follows: Section II reviews relevant literature in the field. clean_text: We compare the performance of our model against two main model categories: sequence models and dense mixer models, that are purely data-driven and exclude any priors of the network. We aim to cover a comprehensive selection of data-driven learning architectures, suitable for the state estimation task at hand. We use a Mamba-based baseline, which we refer to as Mamba Mixer , in a seq-to-point mixer configuration which substitutes the state estimator we use in our MambaDSSE with a decoder that directly transforms latent space representations into system states outputs. Similarly, we use an LSTM , first as a residual recurrent neural network baseline (referred to as LSTM henceforth), and second, as an in-place variation of the selective SSM. This baseline, which we refer to as LSTM+SE , is the same configuration as our MambaDSSE model, except with the Mamba backbone replaced with an LSTM.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary difference between the Mamba Mixer baseline and the LSTM+SE baseline in terms of their state estimation approach?
finetune_sample.output: The primary difference between the Mamba Mixer baseline and the LSTM+SE baseline lies in their state estimation approach. The Mamba Mixer uses a seq-to-point mixer configuration, where the state estimator is replaced with a decoder that directly transforms latent space representations into system states outputs. In contrast, the LSTM+SE baseline uses an in-place variation of the selective SSM, replacing the Mamba backbone with an LSTM, but retains the same state estimation approach as the MambaDSSE model.

Baselines. To strictly isolate the initialization bottleneck, all methods share an identical Qwen3-0.6B backbone and RQ-VAE tokenization structure, differing only in how they introduce novel tokens. 
- (1) Vanilla SFT : New SID tokens are mean-initialized (Eq. 1), inducing a semantic collapse. The model relies entirely on downstream fine-tuning to disambiguate tokens from this degenerate starting point.
- (2) LC-Rec (Zheng et al., 2024): A recent multi-task approach that begins from the same collapsed state but attempts to recover semantic structure by applying auxiliary natural language alignment objectives during fi ne-tuning.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the main issue that the authors of the Grounded Token Initialization Hypothesis are trying to address with their proposed methodology?
finetune_sample.output: The authors of the Grounded Token Initialization Hypothesis are trying to address the problem of token initialization collapsing all new tokens into a degenerate subspace, erasing inter-token distinctions that subsequent fine-tuning struggles to fully recover. This collapse can lead to poor performance in generative recommendation tasks.

clean_text:
Through spectral and geometric diagnostics, we show that mean-of-vocabulary initialization collapses new tokens into a degenerate subspace that fine-tuning does not fully recover. Motivated by this diagnosis, we propose GTI , a lightweight grounding stage that learns only 
the new token embeddings via paired linguistic supervision before standard fine-tuning. On generative recommendation benchmarks spanning industrial-scale and public datasets, GTI consistently outperforms both mean initialization and auxiliary-task adaptation, with further analyses confirming that grounded structure persists through fine-tuning. These findings support the Grounded Token Initialization Hypothesis. As the grounding mechanism makes no assumptions about the downstream task, an important direction for future work is to test its generality in broader vocabulary-extension settings beyond recommendation.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the specific mechanism by which GTI maps new tokens to distinct, semantically meaningful locations in the pretrained embedding space?
finetune_sample.output: GTI maps new tokens to distinct, semantically meaningful locations in the pretrained embedding space using only paired linguistic supervision. This process involves learning the new token embeddings without making assumptions about the downstream task.

clean_text:
Metrics. CRPS (lower better): continuous ranked probability score computed from quantile forecasts (10th, 50th, 90th percentiles) over the forecast horizon and variables, then averaged. MSE (standardized, lower better): mean squared error between point forecasts (median) and targets in standardized space. Both are reported per (dataset, model, horizon); the main table (Table 3) shows horizon-averaged values per dataset; the full table (Table 5) reports all horizons. 
Protocol. Common settings: window = 96 , stride = 25 , and up to 10 rolling forecast origins per (dataset, horizon). Each model is trained once per (dataset, horizon); metrics are aggregated over origins. iVDFM encoder/decoder/prior architecture and training (epochs, learning rate, etc.) are fixed per dataset in the experiment config. Results: Table 3 (main text, horizon-averaged); Table 5 (appendix, all horizons).

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the Identifiable Variational Dynamic Factor Model (iVDFM) ensures identifiability of latent factors up to permutation and component-wise affine transformations?
finetune_sample.output: The iVDFM achieves identifiability by applying iVAE-style conditioning to the innovation process driving the dynamics rather than to the latent states. This approach allows for the identification of factors up to permutation and component-wise affine transformations, which are essential for many applications in time series analysis.

clean_text: leads on ETTh2, ETTm1, ETTm2 and Weather on average; DDFM is strong on Weather. The strong probabilistic performance of TimeMixer and iVDFM may reflect their structural inductive biases: TimeMixer's decomposable multiscale mixing (Wang et al., 2024a) separates and recombines past segments at multiple scales, which can help capture uncertainty at different horizons; iVDFM models uncertainty explicitly at the innovation level and propagates it through factor dynamics, yielding a proper generative predictive distribution. iVDFM does not dominate point or probabilistic metrics here, consistent with its primary design for identifiable dynamics and intervenability rather than raw forecast accuracy. The results show that the learned representation remains useful for downstream forecasting while retaining the interpretability and causal structure exploited in experiments (1) and (2).

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how iVDFM models uncertainty explicitly at the innovation level and propagates it through factor dynamics, yielding a proper generative predictive distribution?
finetune_sample.output: iVDFM models uncertainty explicitly at the innovation level by applying iVAE-style conditioning to the innovation process driving the dynamics. This conditioning allows the model to propagate uncertainty through the factor dynamics, resulting in a proper generative predictive distribution that captures both the structural dynamics and the inherent uncertainty of the data.

clean_text: Cross-attention gate as a continuous control knob between ViT and SteerViT. The tanh-gated cross-attention mechanism (Eq. (2)) provides an implicit control knob for text conditioning strength. At inference, we can scale the learned gating parameters α ℓ by a factor ω ∈ [0 , 1] to smoothly interpolate between the unaltered ViT subspace and a fully text-conditioned state. Plotting this trajectory (Fig. 2, Fig. 7) reveals a clear Pareto frontier with an optimal operating point at a scaling factor of ω =0 . 6 for DINOv2 and SigLIP, where both slightly exceed the original ViT's representation quality while unlocking high steerability. Most strikingly, for MAE, representation quality monotonically improves as ω increases, rising from 40 at ω =0 . 0 to 50 points at ω =0 . 6 . Text conditioning enriches MAE's features with semantic structure, making them more transferable.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the purpose of using a tanh-gated cross-attention mechanism in Steerable Visual Representations?
finetune_sample.output: The tanh-gated cross-attention mechanism serves as an implicit control knob for text conditioning strength, allowing for smooth interpolation between unaltered ViT subspace and fully text-conditioned state. This enables the model to steer its visual features towards desired objects in an image while preserving representation quality.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the purpose of the GTI (Grounded Token Initialization) stage in the proposed method for extending LMs with new vocabularies?
finetune_sample.output: The GTI stage maps new tokens to distinct, semantically meaningful locations in the pretrained embedding space using only paired linguistic supervision. This stage aims to better enable the model to leverage its general-purpose knowledge for novel-token domains by linguistically grounding novel tokens in the pretrained embedding space before fine-tuning.

The retrieval gallery contains distractors that either share the scene but omit the object or contain the object in a different scene. Despite this more challenging setting, SteerViT transfers well in zero-shot evaluation, reaching 25.4% R@1, compared to 9.6% for DINOv2 and 18.7% for the benchmark's specialized baseline (cf. Although MLLMs offer moderate steerability, they bear significant computational cost and diminished visual feature quality.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary limitation of using Pretrained Vision Transformers like DINOv2 for downstream tasks that require steering towards specific concepts in an image?
finetune_sample.output: Pretrained Vision Transformers such as DINOv2 tend to focus on the most salient visual cues in the image, with no way to direct them toward less prominent concepts of interest. This limitation is due to their generic image features that are not steerable towards specific concepts.

We operationalize this hypothesis as GTI (Grounded Token Initialization), a lightweight grounding stage that, prior to fine-tuning, maps new tokens to distinct, semantically meaningful locations in the pretrained embedding space using only paired linguistic supervision. Despite its simplicity, GTI outperforms both mean initialization and existing auxiliary-task adaptation methods in the majority of evaluation settings across multiple generative recommendation benchmarks, including industry-scale and public datasets. Further analyses show that grounded embeddings produce richer inter-token structure that persists through fine-tuning, corroborating the hypothesis that initialization quality is a key bottleneck in vocabulary extension.

clean_text: We examine whether supervised fine-tuning recovers the structure lost under mean initialization. The pairwise similarity among SID embeddings (Fig.2 (c) and Fig.6 Left&Mid) and the singular-value decomposition of the SID embedding matrix E SID ∈ R |V SID |× d after supervised fine-tuning from the mean-initialized state (Fig.2 (b) and Fig.7) reveals rapid spectral decay and low effective rank, confirming that supervised fine-tuning alone does not recover the inter-token structure lost at mean or random initialization. Taken together, these analyses show that neither strategy provides a suitable starting point: mean initialization places tokens on the pretrained manifold but erases discrimination, while random initialization preserves discrimination but lacks linguistic grounding.

In this paper, we introduce ReVAR (Re-whitened Vector AutoRegression), a data-driven algorithm for generating synthetic data that matches the statistics of measured aero-optic phase screens. ReVAR is computationally efficient for fitting parameters to data and for generating synthetic data. A key component of ReVAR is a linear predictive model that we call Long-Range AutoRegression, which includes the following novel contributions: 
- A multiple time-lag AR model to generate synthetic aero-optic phase screen data that matches high-frequency temporal correlations of both the measured data and the streamwise slopes of the measured data.
- Multiple low-pass filters used to augment this AR model in order to match the long-range temporal correlations of the measured data.

finetune_sample.output: The technical excerpt does not specify the exact type of low-pass filters used, but it mentions that multiple low-pass filters are employed to augment the AR model and match the long-range temporal correlations of the measured data.

clean_text: Baselines. Since there is no open-source method capable of controlling more than two subjects, we design several baselines. We use the Wan2.1-14B I2V model , which is much larger than the 1.3B model ActionParty fine-tunes from. We also study such Text-Action control on our autoregressive setup, using the same backbone as ActionParty, but trained only using textual conditioning. Finally, we take the autoregressive model pretrained on game videos without action control (termed Pretrained AR ) as a random action lower bound.

clean_text:
1. Diagnosis. Through spectral and geometric analysis, we characterize the tokenembedding misalignment caused by mean initialization: all new learnable tokens collapse into a degenerate, low-rank subspace that does not fully recover under subsequent fine-tuning. This motivates the Grounded Token Initialization Hypothesis : linguistically grounding new tokens before fine-tuning better enables the LM to leverage its pretrained knowledge for the new domain.
2. Methodology. Weintroduce GTI , a simple and effective grounding stage that freezes the LM backbone and learns new token embeddings via paired linguistic supervision before standard fine-tuning, providing a semantically structured starting point for downstream adaptation.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary motivation behind the Grounded Token Initialization Hypothesis, as proposed in the paper?
finetune_sample.output: The primary motivation behind the Grounded Token Initialization Hypothesis is that mean initialization collapses all new tokens into a degenerate subspace, erasing inter-token distinctions that subsequent fine-tuning struggles to fully recover. This leads to the hypothesis that linguistically grounding novel tokens in the pretrained embedding space before fine-tuning better enables the model to leverage its general-purpose knowledge for novel-token domains.

clean_text: Specifically, a video token at position p = ( h, w ) in frame x t receives a rotation R ( t, h, w ) . Given the subject positions from the previous timestep z i t -1 = ( h i t -1 , w i t -1 ) , we apply a rotation R ( t, h i t -1 , w i t -1 ) to the subject token z i t . We utilize the previous spatial position because the current position z i t is being denoised and thus unknown at the current stage. Still, z i t -1 should be close to the true z i t , ensuring that video tokens close to the subject are biased to attend to the corresponding subject state token. Consequently, the model's task is greatly simplified from global disambiguation to local refinement within a single action's radius, as illustrated in Fig. 3a.

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how ActionParty handles subject state tokens in its video diffusion model, and what is the purpose of using previous spatial positions?
finetune_sample.output: ActionParty handles subject state tokens by jointly modeling them with video latents using a spatial biasing mechanism. This approach disentangles global video frame rendering from individual action-controlled subject updates. By utilizing the previous spatial position, the model can ensure that video tokens close to the subject attend to the corresponding subject state token, simplifying the task of local refinement within a single action's radius.

clean_text:
Visual representation families. We summarize popular approaches against our desiderata in Tab. 1 and compare their architectures in Fig. 3. Unimodal self-supervised encoders (DINOv2 , MAE ) learn rich visual features but are inherently query-agnostic. Cross-modal encoders (CLIP , SigLIP , CoCoOp ) use text to provide training supervision; the visual encoder still cannot be steered with text. MLLMs come closest, offering moderate steerability and visual quality, but their representations reside in language space and require 
billions of parameters. SteerViT inverts this paradigm: we condition vision on language, add only ∼ 21M trainable parameters to a frozen ViT, while yielding stronger visual features (Fig. 2).

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the main difference between Steerable Visual Representations and other vision-language models?
finetune_sample.output: Steerable Visual Representations differ from other vision-language models in that they condition the visual encoder directly on language, allowing for steerable visual features without requiring billions of parameters. This approach is distinct from late fusion methods like CLIP, which fuse text with visual features after encoding.

clean_text: Connection to Dimensional Collapse. The initialization collapse we diagnose is related to dimensional collapse in contrastive and self-supervised learning (Jing et al., 2021; Jiang et al., 2024), where learned representations are restricted to a low-dimensional subspace, eliminating fine-grained distinctions (Figure 2). Mean-of-vocabulary initialization induces a similar effect: all new tokens start at the same point, forming a rank-deficient configuration. Jiang et al. (2024) show that appropriate initialization can mitigate dimensional collapse in contrastive learning, which parallels our finding that grounding new tokens before fine-tuning preserves a higher-rank, more differentiated embedding subspace.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the relationship between the proposed Grounded Token Initialization (GTI) method and dimensional collapse in contrastive and self-supervised learning?
finetune_sample.output: The proposed GTI method aims to mitigate dimensional collapse by grounding new tokens in a distinct, semantically meaningful location within the pretrained embedding space. This approach preserves a higher-rank, more differentiated embedding subspace, which contrasts with the effect of mean-of-vocabulary initialization that collapses all new tokens into a degenerate subspace.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary challenge faced by existing intrusion detection systems in capturing the structure of modern adversarial campaigns?
finetune_sample.output: Existing intrusion detection systems struggle to capture the structure of modern adversarial campaigns because signature-based methods cannot detect zero-day attacks, deep-learning models provide opaque anomaly scores without stage attribution, and standard Kalman Filters cannot model non-stationary multi-modal dynamics.

clean_text: Our predicted position z t may not completely align with the subject after a wrong move, but our smooth position biasing allows actions to still bind to correct subjects. Some subjects can disappear, which prevents further interaction with the scene. Our model is also not fully real-time yet, however with techniques such as distillation and diffusion forcing , the model can be made more interactive. The main focus of this work is on action binding. We test our method in a 2D game setup as it aligns well with the task of binding actions to multiple subjects within the same scene. Further work may be done to extend this to other game environments like 3D scenes where subjects are partially visible .

clean_text: Protocol and optimization. Aligning evaluation with benchmarks (rolling stride equal to horizon, max origins 10) improved MSE. Dropout 0.08 was slightly better than 0.10. Layer normalization and a uniform encoder/decoder (2 layers, 128 hidden dim) are retained. OLS initialization for AR coefficients (from PCA factors) gave a small improvement; single-window PCA for f 0 remains default. Regime temperature τ = 0 . 2 (num regimes=7) and factor order 2-3 (dataset-specific) complete the main configuration.

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain why the authors chose to apply iVAE-style conditioning to the innovation process driving the dynamics rather than to the latent states in their Identifiable Variational Dynamic Factor Model (iVDFM)?
finetune_sample.output: The authors applied iVAE-style conditioning to the innovation process driving the dynamics because this approach allows for identifiability guarantees up to permutation and component-wise affine transformations. By conditioning on the innovations, they can ensure that the latent factors are identifiable even when subject to certain types of transformations or perturbations.

clean_text:
The diagnosis in Section 2 motivates a straightforward modification to the standard training pipeline: before downstream fine-tuning, insert a grounding stage that freezes the LM backbone and only learns new token embeddings via paired linguistic supervision. This design builds on the established principle of training new token embeddings within a frozen LM (Hao et al., 2024; Nguyen et al., 2024). We term the resulting procedure GTI . Despite its simplicity, we show that this additional stage yields consistent improvements 
across multiple benchmarks, including both public and industry-scale datasets (Section 4), suggesting that token initialization is a key bottleneck in vocabulary extension.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the main motivation behind proposing Grounded Token Initialization (GTI) as a modification to the standard training pipeline for language models?
finetune_sample.output: The main motivation behind proposing GTI is that mean initialization collapses all new tokens into a degenerate subspace, erasing inter-token distinctions that subsequent fine-tuning struggles to fully recover. This suggests that token initialization is a key bottleneck when extending LMs with new vocabularies.

clean_text:
Rendering in self-attention. To render video frames, we apply a mask M SA in DiT self-attention layers that encourages subject isolation while enabling visual integration. This mask allows all subject tokens to attend to the video tokens, enabling them to read information from the environment. Yet, we block subjectto-subject communication to avoid mixing the state between different subjects. On the other hand, the video tokens attend to all subject tokens, allowing the model to use the state of all subjects for frame synthesis. 
Subject-binding with RoPE bias. While the self-attention mask M SA prevents subject mixing, it does not explicitly link a subject in the video to its state

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the subject-to-subject communication is blocked in ActionParty's rendering process?
finetune_sample.output: In ActionParty, subject-to-subject communication is blocked by applying a mask M SA in DiT self-attention layers during video frame rendering. This mask allows all subject tokens to attend to the video tokens while preventing them from attending to each other, thereby avoiding mixing of states between different subjects.

clean_text: Grounded Token Initialization ( GTI ) Hypothesis. These observations motivate our central hypothesis: linguistically grounding novel tokens in an LM's pretrained embedding space, before downstream fine-tuning, better enables the model to leverage its general-purpose knowledge for novel-token domains. Rather than relying on fine-tuning alone to recover from a degenerate initialization, we propose inserting a simple and efficient grounding stage that learns new token embeddings via linguistic supervision with the backbone frozen, before proceeding to standard end-to-end fine-tuning. We operationalize this hypothesis in Section 3 and verify its effectiveness empirically in Section 4.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the main motivation behind proposing Grounded Token Initialization (GTI), a new methodology for initializing new vocabulary tokens in LMs?
finetune_sample.output: Grounded Token Initialization (GTI) is proposed to address the issue of degenerate initialization, where mean initialization collapses all new tokens into a subspace, erasing inter-token distinctions. GTI aims to linguistically ground novel tokens in the pretrained embedding space using paired linguistic supervision, enabling the model to leverage its general-purpose knowledge for novel-token domains.

clean_text:
A fundamental limitation of deploying Deep Learning models for real-time Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) is the latency introduced by user-space context switching and the Python Global Interpreter Lock (GIL). To achieve true line-rate inference, AEGIS completely decouples network ingestion from neural execution. 
We implement a split-brain architecture. A pure C++ Harvester utilizes Extended Berkeley Packet Filter (eBPF) via libbpf to intercept traffic directly at the network interface card (NIC) using eXpress Data Path (XDP). The eBPF Sentry extracts the 6-dimensional physics vector x i and executes Direct Memory Access (DMA) into a pre-allocated physical RAM block ( /dev/shm ).

finetune_sample.instruction: How does AEGIS's split-brain architecture address the limitations of traditional Deep Learning models for real-time Deep Packet Inspection (DPI)?
finetune_sample.output: AEGIS's split-brain architecture decouples network ingestion from neural execution by utilizing a pure C++ Harvester that intercepts traffic directly at the network interface card (NIC) using Extended Berkeley Packet Filter (eBPF). This approach eliminates user-space context switching and the Python Global Interpreter Lock (GIL), enabling true line-rate inference. By executing the eBPF Sentry in Direct Memory Access (DMA) into a pre-allocated physical RAM block, AEGIS achieves a significant reduction in latency, allowing for real-time analysis of encrypted traffic.

Fig. 1. Overview of the MambaDSSE framework. Historical system data is used to train the model to infer the underlying time-varying behavior of the observed system. The input data is lifted into a high-dimensional latent space via learned Koopman observables, to linearize the complex nonlinear dynamics. The Mamba-based matrix generation engine produces the state transition matrices that drive a probabilistic state estimation filter that's responsible for modeling the measurement noise and process uncertainty. A decoding pipeline, conditioned on the posterior state distribution of the filter, produces the final state predictions.

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the Koopman-theoretic probabilistic filtering works in the MambaDSSE framework?
finetune_sample.output: The Koopman-theoretic probabilistic filtering in the MambaDSSE framework uses learned Koopman observables to linearize complex nonlinear dynamics and lift input data into a high-dimensional latent space. This allows for the estimation of the underlying time-varying behavior of the system, which is then used to drive a probabilistic state estimation filter that models measurement noise and process uncertainty. The filter produces a posterior state distribution, which is conditioned on to produce final state predictions through a decoding pipeline.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the main difference between late fusion and early fusion in the context of vision-language models?
finetune_sample.output: In the context of vision-language models, late fusion involves fusing text with visual features after encoding, whereas early fusion involves injecting text directly into the layers of the visual encoder. The main difference is that early fusion allows for more control over the direction of the visual representation, enabling it to focus on specific objects or concepts in an image while preserving its underlying quality.

clean_text: Late fusion does not enable steerability. Although CLIP and SigLIP operate in a shared vision-language space, their visual features are extracted independently of the query. Post-hoc element-wise addition of text yields a negligible 0.02% boost over their vision-only representations, confirming that late fusion cannot steer frozen visual features. In contrast, by conditioning intermediate representations on text (i.e., early fusion), SteerViT improves retrieval accuracy from 43.7% (vanilla DINOv2) to 96.0%. While FLAIR's trained attention pooling offers greater steerability (81.3%) than typical cross-modal encoders, it still falls short of SteerViT by 14.7 points.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary limitation of late fusion in vision-language models compared to early fusion, as demonstrated by SteerViT?
finetune_sample.output: Late fusion does not enable steerability because it extracts visual features independently of the query, resulting in a negligible boost over vision-only representations. In contrast, early fusion allows for conditioning intermediate representations on text, enabling steerability and improving retrieval accuracy.

(1) The output is integrated into the residual stream through a tanh gate with a layer-specific learnable scalar α ℓ , initialized to zero: 
<!-- formula-not-decoded --> 
Since tanh(0) = 0 , the model is identical to the frozen ViT at initialization. Despite the zero-initialization, the gate still receives a learning signal since ∂Z ( ℓ +1) v ∂α ℓ = sech 2 ( α ℓ ) · ˆ Z ( ℓ ) v , and sech 2 (0) = 1 . finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the tanh gate with a layer-specific learnable scalar α ℓ is used to steer the visual representations in the Steerable Visual Representations method?
finetune_sample.output: The tanh gate with a layer-specific learnable scalar α ℓ is used to integrate the output of the cross-attention mechanism into the residual stream. The gate is applied to the output of the layer using the formula tanh(α ℓ) * Z(ℓ+1)v, where Z(ℓ+1)v represents the feature map at layer ℓ+1. This allows the model to selectively focus on certain parts of the image while preserving the overall representation quality. The learnable scalar α ℓ is initialized to zero and can move away from zero during optimization, gradually activating the conditioning pathway and allowing the model to incorporate language-based clues.

clean_text: Building on this hypothesis, we introduce GTI , a simple and effective grounded token initialization method. Before downstream fine-tuning, GTI freezes the LM backbone and grounds newly introduced token embeddings using paired supervision between natural language descriptions and the corresponding new tokens (Figure 1). This grounding stage resolves the mismatch between well-trained vocabulary embeddings and newly initialized tokens, providing the LM with a semantically structured starting point for subsequent end-to-end fine-tuning of the full model for target downstream tasks.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the main issue that the authors of the paper are trying to address by proposing Grounded Token Initialization Hypothesis?
finetune_sample.output: The main issue addressed by the Grounded Token Initialization Hypothesis is that standard token initialization methods, such as initializing new tokens as the mean of existing vocabulary embeddings, can collapse all new tokens into a degenerate subspace, erasing inter-token distinctions. This makes it difficult for subsequent fine-tuning to fully recover the representations of individual tokens.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary challenge that ActionParty aims to address in existing video diffusion models, and how does it differ from their single-agent settings?
finetune_sample.output: ActionParty tackles a fundamental issue of action binding in existing video diffusion models, which struggle to associate specific actions with their corresponding subjects. This limitation is primarily due to the lack of control over multiple agents simultaneously in a scene. ActionParty introduces subject state tokens to persistently capture the state of each subject in the scene, allowing for disentanglement of global video frame rendering from individual action-controlled subject updates.

finetune_sample.output: Existing intrusion detection systems fail to capture this structure due to their inability to model non-stationary multi-modal dynamics, which are characteristic of modern adversarial campaigns unfolding as sequences of behavioural phases. finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the OpenHummingBird evaluation methodology works for binary object-of-interest segmentation?
finetune_sample.output: The OpenHummingBird evaluation methodology involves precomputing patch-class pairs for the training split of the ADE20k dataset. At inference, patch-wise segmentation is performed by finding the nearest-neighbor retrieval from a reference bank, which allows for efficient and accurate evaluation of patch-level features. This approach enables separate evaluation of each class, conditioning features on the original class name of the object of interest.

finetune_sample.output: Steerable visual representations refer to a new class of image features that can be directed or steered towards specific concepts or objects of interest using natural language prompts. This allows for more precise and targeted feature extraction, which can improve performance on various downstream tasks such as retrieval, classification, and segmentation.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the main challenge that existing intrusion detection systems face in detecting modern adversarial campaigns?
finetune_sample.output: Existing intrusion detection systems struggle to capture the structure of modern adversarial campaigns, which unfold as sequences of behavioral phases indistinguishable from legitimate traffic when viewed in isolation. This makes it difficult for signature-based methods to detect zero-day attacks and deep-learning models to provide stage attribution.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the primary issue with using mean initialization for new vocabulary tokens in LMs, according to the authors of the paper?
finetune_sample.output: The standard practice of initializing new tokens as the mean of existing vocabulary embeddings collapses all new tokens into a degenerate subspace, erasing inter-token distinctions that subsequent fine-tuning struggles to fully recover. This is because mean initialization fails to capture the semantic meaning and relationships between tokens, leading to suboptimal representations for novel tokens.

finetune_sample.instruction: What is the key difference between late fusion and early fusion in the Steerable Visual Representations approach?
finetune_sample.output: In the Steerable Visual Representations approach, the key difference between late fusion and early fusion lies in when text is injected into the visual encoder. Late fusion involves fusing text with visual features after encoding, whereas early fusion injects text directly into the layers of the visual encoder via lightweight cross-attention. This allows for precise control over the granularity of visual features through the level of detail in the text prompt.

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how subject state tokens persistently capture the state of each subject in the scene?

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the exploratory framework of Wang et al. [2020] handles the case where the stopping times are modeled by jump times of Cox processes driven by bounded, non-randomized intensity controls?
finetune_sample.output: The exploratory framework of Wang et al. [2020] models the stopping times as jump times of Cox processes driven by bounded, non-randomized intensity controls in a relaxed version of the sequential optimal stopping problem. This approach allows for a more tractable and solvable formulation of the problem, enabling the derivation of closed-form solutions for the optimal policy using HJB equations and Gibbs distributions. By regularizing the agent's randomized control via Shannon's differential entropy, the framework provides a robust and efficient method for finding entry-and-exit strategies in speculative trading applications.

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the Virtual-Variable-Length method alleviates adverse behaviors and constraints in inverse kinematics of multi-segment continuum robots?
finetune_sample.output: The Virtual-Variable-Length (VVL) method introduces fictitious variations of segments' length during the solution iteration, conferring virtual axial degrees of freedom. This allows the method to alleviate adverse behaviors and constraints that can occur in inverse kinematics, such as slow convergence or deadlocks, by providing additional flexibility and reducing the likelihood of reaching poses where the manipulator is constrained or at a boundary. By doing so, the VVL method enables or accelerates convergence to solutions, improving the overall robustness and efficiency of the inverse kinematics process.

clean_text: Abstract -This paper proposes a novel Mean-Field Game (MFG) framework for large-scale attacker-defender systems aimed at protecting one or multiple High-Value Units (HVUs). Motivated by classical agent-wise attrition models, we introduce a population-wise attrition mechanism formulated by statistical distance between populations, enabling a macroscopic description of weapon-based interactions between large populations. Leveraging this and Lions derivative on the space of probability measures, we derive the associated MFG system, which characterizes optimal strategies and the evolution of population distributions in attacker-defender interactions. We analyze the model by establishing upper and lower bounds on the defender density, ensuring physical consistency by preventing concentration and depletion. For numerical investigation, we develop a numerical scheme combining physics-informed neural networks with Sinkhorn method to solve attacker-defender MFG system. Simulations confirm the

This means that the rate at which individuals are lost or 'killed' in the system is determined by the difference between the probability distributions of the attacker and defender populations.

clean_text: Abstract: The propagation of light through a turbulent flow field around an aircraft results in optical distortions commonly known as aero-optic effects. The development of methods to mitigate these effects requires large amounts of realistic aero-optic data. However, methods for obtaining this data, including experiment, computational fluid dynamics, and simple phase screen algorithms (e.g., boiling flow), each have significant drawbacks such as high cost, high computation, limited quantity, and/or inaccurate statistics. More recently, data-driven algorithms have been proposed that are computationally efficient and can synthesize aero-optic data to match the statistics of measured data, but these approaches still have drawbacks including limited quality, inaccurate statistics, and the use of complicated algorithms. clean_text: Abstract -This paper proposes a new, robust method to solve the inverse kinematics (IK) of multi-segment continuum manipulators. Conventional Jacobian-based solvers, especially when initialized from neutral/rest configurations, often exhibit slow convergence and, in certain conditions, may fail to converge (deadlock). The Virtual-Variable-Length (VVL) method proposed here introduces fictitious variations of segments' length during the solution iteration, conferring virtual axial degrees of freedom that alleviate adverse behaviors and constraints, thus enabling or accelerating convergence. Comprehensive numerical experiments were conducted to compare the VVL method against benchmark Jacobian-based and Damped Least Square IK solvers. Across more than 1.8 × 10 6 randomized trials covering manipulators with two to seven segments, the proposed approach achieved up to a 20% increase in convergence success rate over the benchmark and a 40-80% reduction in average iteration count under

finetune_sample.instruction: Can you explain how the Virtual-Variable-Length method alleviates adverse behaviors and constraints in conventional Jacobian-based solvers during inverse kinematics solution iteration?
finetune_sample.output: The Virtual-Variable-Length (VVL) method introduces fictitious variations of segments' length during the solution iteration, conferring virtual axial degrees of freedom. This alleviates adverse behaviors and constraints by providing additional flexibility in the system, thus enabling or accelerating convergence. By introducing these fictitious variations, the VVL method mitigates issues such as deadlocks that can occur when conventional Jacobian-based solvers are initialized from neutral/rest configurations.

clean_text: Eigenanalysis and spectral analysis in the Koopman framework enables us to understand the behavior of the observed nonlinear system. Dynamic Mode Decomposition (DMD) is often used to identify approximate finite-dimensional Koopman operator representations based on linear measurements, where the Extended DMD lifts the linear assumptions and allows the model to process nonlinear measurements. Their performance, however, is bottlenecked by the manual selection of a set of basis functions. Finding such functions that can appropriately represent the complex dynamics remains an open problem in itself . Data-driven representation learning methods instead utilize end-to-end learning of the lifting transformations to discover nonlinear embeddings to linearize complex systems, strictly from data without knowledge of the system governing equations. Recent data-driven methods make use of Koopman theory, leveraging measurement function learning, to decompose nonlinear dynamics into linear

finetune_sample.output: Extended DMD lifts the linear assumptions in DMD by allowing the model to process nonlinear measurements, enabling it to handle complex dynamics that classical DMD may struggle with. This is achieved through the use of lifting transformations, which are learned end-to-end from data without prior knowledge of the system governing equations.

Interestingly, in the three-segment case, the DLS method significantly improves the convergence success rate compared to the benchmark Jacobian approach and the VVL method. finetune_sample.instruction: What specific conditions lead to deadlocks in the inverse kinematics of multi-segment continuum robots, according to the provided text?

The proposed Virtual-Variable-Length (VVL) method mitigates such occurrences by introducing fictitious variations of segments' length during solution iteration.

clean_text: The construction of the coding scheme follows the structure of , and is inspired by random binning , , which leverages source coding with side information and privacy amplification via hash functions. The key insight is that a source coding protocol can be used as a building block for a channel coding scheme (Lemma 3), provided the channel input is uniformly distributed. Since the optimal input distribution is generally not uniform, we create a function, referred to as shaper , using 2-universal hash functions, which maps an almost uniform random variable to one with an arbitrary distribution. The leftover hash lemma (Lemma 1) is the key tool to both approximate the desired input distribution from a uniform distribution and secure the secret. Defining a virtual channel W ′ as the composition of the shaper and the channel W , gives us a channel with almost uniform input. We then design a source coding protocol for the virtual channel W ′ (Lemma 2), which is then used to

finetune_sample.output: The shaper function uses 2-universal hash functions to map an almost uniform random variable to one with an arbitrary distribution. This is done to approximate the desired input distribution from a uniform distribution, which is not generally optimal for channel coding. By creating a virtual channel W' as the composition of the shaper and the channel W, we can design a source coding protocol that takes advantage of this approximation to secure the secret.
